tool.name: VBET

param.name: dem
param.dialogReference: Digital Elevation Model (DEM) provides elevation data for the program.

param.name: z_unit
param.dialogReference:
Z-unit of raster vertical coordinate system

This can be set using Data Management/Define Projection

param.name: rem
param.dialogReference:
Relative elevation model (REM) showing height of land above nearest drainage

This can be calculated using Terrain Analysis/Relative Elevation Model (REM)

param.name: stream
param.dialogReference:
Stream feature class

A hydrologically accurate to DEM stream feature class can be calculated using Terrain Modification/Burn Culverts into DEM and then calculating stream lines with Fluvial Geomorphology/Calculate Stream Network

param.name: watershed_size_field
param.dialogReference: Stream feature class field containing watershed size information. This can be added automatically to your stream lines created with Fluvial Geomorphology/Calculate Stream Network

param.name: unit
param.expression: Unknown | Square International Inches | Square US Inches | Square International Feet | Square US Feet | Square International Yards | Square US Yards | International Acres | US Survey Acres | Square Statute Miles | Square US Survey Miles | Square Millimeters | Square Centimeters | Square Decimeters | Square Meters | Square Kilometers | Ares | Hectares

param.name: buffer_radius
param.dialogReference: Radius away from stream line to look for potential valley bottom areas

param.name: min_watershed
param.dialogReference: Minimum watershed size of stream to analyze for valley bottom extent

param.name: valley_bottom
param.dialogReference: Valley bottom output feature. Likelihood > 0.65, see tool description for likelihood calculation information

param.name: low_lying
param.dialogReference: Low-lying valley bottom output feature. Likelihood > 0.85, see tool description for likelihood calculation information

tool.summary: Implementation of the Valley Bottom Extract Tool originally defined by Gilbert et al 2016. It finds valley bottoms by calculating low slope low relative elevation landforms along streams. The VBET tool has varying thresholds based off of watershed size as described here.

searchKeys.keyword: Terrain Analysis Valley Bottom Extract Tool VBET V-BET Floodplain DEM
